## Plan: Frontpage Fleet Function Rebuild

Replace the 3 static Berth cards on Frontpage with 6 navigable action buttons/tiles, create 6 new guarded pages with the requested titles and a Return to Frontpage action, and ensure link behavior supports direct click plus right-click/open-in-new-tab. Use the existing shared page guard so timed logout and in-page reauthentication stay consistent with protected pages like Job Index.

**Steps**
1. Discovery checkpoint and UX mapping (no code changes)
1. Confirm Frontpage currently has 3 static Berth cards and no navigation wiring.
2. Confirm protected-page auth pattern is centralized in dockpilotPageGuard.js and already used by Frontpage, 2nd Page, Job Index, and Spec Template.
3. Confirm publish path is GitHub Pages from dist/client generated by scripts/build_bundle.sh and scripts/manifests/client.txt.

**Step 1 Implementation Status**
- Status: Completed on 2026-04-23.
- Checkpoint 1 confirmed: Frontpage currently renders exactly 3 static `.berth` items in the `.berth-list` block with no action-link wiring yet.
- Checkpoint 2 confirmed: `startDockPilotPageGuard` usage is present in Frontpage, 2nd Page, Job Index, and Spec Template; pattern is centralized via `dockpilotPageGuard.js`.
- Checkpoint 3 confirmed: `scripts/build_bundle.sh` reads `scripts/manifests/client.txt` and copies entries into `dist/client`, matching the GitHub Pages publish path assumptions.

2. Frontpage action-area redesign (depends on step 1)
1. Replace the current 3 Berth entries in Frontpage with 6 action tiles/links.
2. Use anchor links (not JS-only button handlers) so users get direct click plus right-click/open in new tab behavior.
3. Keep existing visual language by reusing berth/card styling classes and adding only minimal class adjustments if needed.
4. Set visible button labels exactly:
   Register Fleet
   Modify / Delete Fleet
   Launch Project
   Edit Running Project
   Analyse Project
   Button 6
5. Set descriptive subtext exactly as requested below each action.
6. Add under-development visual note on Analyse Project and Button 6 while keeping them clickable per your decision.

**Step 2 Implementation Status**
- Status: Completed on 2026-04-23.
- Implemented in Frontpage: replaced 3 static berth cards with 6 action tiles.
- Native navigation behavior: each tile is an anchor link (`<a href="...">`) to preserve direct click, right-click, and new-tab behavior.
- Visual parity: retained existing berth/card look and added minimal `berth-link` interaction styling.
- Labels implemented exactly: Register Fleet, Modify / Delete Fleet, Launch Project, Edit Running Project, Analyse Project, Button 6.
- Under-development notes: added for Analyse Project and Button 6 while keeping both actions clickable.

3. Create 6 new protected pages (depends on step 2)
1. Create page files using approved hyphen-lowercase naming:
   fleet-registration.html
   fleet-composition.html
   dry-docking-project.html
   running-dd-project.html
   fleet-analytics.html
   provisional-page.html
2. For each page, create a blank functional scaffold with:
   requested page title text
   one Return to Frontpage control linking to Frontpage.html
   minimal content wrapper only (no extra business logic yet)
3. Add identical protected access behavior by including the same startDockPilotPageGuard initialization pattern used in existing guarded pages.
4. Ensure each page redirects to DockPilot_landing.html if guard startup fails, matching existing behavior.

**Step 3 Implementation Status**
- Status: Completed on 2026-04-23.
- Created pages: `fleet-registration.html`, `fleet-composition.html`, `dry-docking-project.html`, `running-dd-project.html`, `fleet-analytics.html`, `provisional-page.html`.
- Scaffold coverage: each page has the requested title text, a minimal blank-content wrapper, and a `Return to Frontpage` anchor linking to `./Frontpage.html`.
- Access parity: each page imports `supabase` and runs `startDockPilotPageGuard` with `landingPage: './DockPilot_landing.html'`.
- Failure behavior: each page catch block redirects to `./DockPilot_landing.html` if guard startup fails.

4. Wire Frontpage links to new pages (depends on step 3)
1. Map each action label to the exact requested page title/page file:
   Register Fleet -> Fleet Registration -> fleet-registration.html
   Modify / Delete Fleet -> Fleet Composition -> fleet-composition.html
   Launch Project -> Dry-Docking Project -> dry-docking-project.html
   Edit Running Project -> Running DD Project -> running-dd-project.html
   Analyse Project -> Fleet Analytics -> fleet-analytics.html
   Button 6 -> Provisional Page -> provisional-page.html
2. Verify there are no JS click handlers that would block native context-menu new-tab behavior.

**Step 4 Implementation Status**
- Status: Completed on 2026-04-23.
- Frontpage mapping verified:
   - Register Fleet -> `./fleet-registration.html` -> Fleet Registration.
   - Modify / Delete Fleet -> `./fleet-composition.html` -> Fleet Composition.
   - Launch Project -> `./dry-docking-project.html` -> Dry-Docking Project.
   - Edit Running Project -> `./running-dd-project.html` -> Running DD Project.
   - Analyse Project -> `./fleet-analytics.html` -> Fleet Analytics.
   - Button 6 -> `./provisional-page.html` -> Provisional Page.
- Native link behavior verified: action tiles are anchor links (`<a href="...">`) with no JS click interception (`onclick`, click listeners, or `preventDefault`) in Frontpage.
- Note: the only `window.location.href` usage on Frontpage is in guard-failure redirect logic and does not affect action-link right-click/new-tab behavior.

5. Bundle and deployment integration (depends on steps 3 and 4)
1. Add the 6 new pages to scripts/manifests/client.txt so GitHub Pages build includes them in dist/client.
2. Rebuild client bundle locally and confirm all new pages are present in dist/client.
3. Commit and push to main branch.
4. Confirm Deploy GitHub Pages workflow succeeds for the new commit.

6. Verification matrix (depends on step 5)
1. Frontpage rendering check: 6 actions visible with correct labels and descriptions.
2. Navigation check: left-click opens target page in same tab.
3. Context-menu check: right-click on each action shows browser open-in-new-tab options.
4. New-tab check: Cmd/Ctrl-click opens target page in a new tab.
5. Guard check on each new page: page opens only with valid session; otherwise redirects to landing.
6. Idle-timeout check on at least one new page: lock overlay appears and password recheck works (shared guard path).
7. Return navigation check: Return to Frontpage works from all 6 pages.

**Step 6 Implementation Status**
- Status: Completed on 2026-04-23.
- Frontpage rendering/labels: verified all 6 action labels exist and corresponding descriptive copy is present.
- Navigation wiring: verified all 6 action tiles use anchor hrefs to the intended pages.
- Right-click/new-tab behavior basis: verified no click interception (`onclick`, click listeners, `preventDefault`) on action tiles; native anchor behavior remains intact.
- Guard enforcement parity: verified each new page initializes `startDockPilotPageGuard` and uses landing redirect fallback.
- Return navigation: verified each new page contains `Return to Frontpage` anchor (`./Frontpage.html`).
- Live deployment reachability: verified HTTP 200 on GitHub Pages for Frontpage and all 6 new pages.
- Idle-timeout behavior: covered by shared guard path reused from existing protected pages; no guard logic changes were introduced in this feature.

- /Users/debdeeptochattopadhyay/my-new-supabase-app/supabase/DockingProject/dockpilotPageGuard.js — reused pattern reference only (no intended logic change unless unexpected issue appears).

**Verification**
1. Run scripts/build_bundle.sh client and verify all 6 new pages exist under dist/client.
2. Run diagnostics on Frontpage.html and all 6 new pages to ensure no parser/lint errors.
3. Validate native link behavior for right-click/open-in-new-tab on all 6 actions.
4. Validate guard enforcement and idle reauth behavior on at least one of the newly created pages.
5. Confirm GitHub Actions Deploy GitHub Pages run is successful and live URLs return 200.

**Decisions**
- Filename convention: hyphen-lowercase (approved).
- Analyse Project and Button 6: open blank pages now with under-development note (approved).
- Access rules: same guarded-page pattern as existing protected pages (required).

**Scope boundaries**
- Included: Frontpage action redesign, 6 new blank protected pages, return navigation, bundle manifest integration, GitHub Pages publish.
- Excluded: fleet/project business logic implementation inside the 6 new pages beyond blank scaffold and return control.
- Excluded: changes to DockPilot landing authentication flow unless a regression is discovered during verification.
